name: Lemon Grove Bundt Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325 degrees Fahrenheit.
2. In a very large bowl, cream butter (or margarine) until very light. Gradually add sugar until the mixture is very light and fluffy. Beat in eggs one at a time.
3. In another bowl, sift flour, baking soda, and salt together. Add flour mixture alternately with buttermilk to butter mixture, beginning and ending with flour. Mix in lemon peel and juice.
4. Pour batter into greased and floured 10-inch tube or 12-inch bundt pan.
5. Bake for 65-75 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean. Place pan on a rack to cool for 10 minutes.
6. Turn cake out of pan onto a rack. Spread with as much lemon icing as the cake can absorb (about one-third of the icing). Allow cake to cool completely, then frost with remaining icing.
Icing:
1. Blend butter and powdered sugar together in a medium bowl until smooth.
2. Mix in grated lemon peel and fresh lemon juice, 1 tablespoon at a time, until the frosting reaches a spreading consistency. If all the lemon juice is used, the icing will be thin and possibly difficult to spread evenly; however, a very tangy frosting greatly enhances the flavor of the cake.
